tasks.title: Implement theme CSS tokens (light + dark) in index.css
tasks.description: Restructure index.css: keep palette constants in @theme, add html.light and html.dark selectors with all semantic token values per the blueprint token table (section 4). Register semantic tokens as Tailwind utilities. Update the base html/body/#root styles to use var(--color-page) and var(--color-text-primary). Verify Tailwind v4 resolves the @theme vars correctly by inspecting generated CSS.
acceptance_criteria: html.light selector defines all 13 semantic color tokens with light values from blueprint table 4.2
html.dark selector defines all 13 semantic color tokens with dark values from blueprint table 4.2
Shadow tokens (card, card-hover, modal) have light and dark variants under their respective selectors

Tailwind classes bg-page, bg-card, text-primary, text-secondary, text-muted, border-border, bg-input, border-input-border, bg-hover, bg-card-name, border-card-border, bg-surface, bg-surface-elevated all resolve correctly
OPTCG palette constants (red, blue, green, yellow, purple, black, gold, navy, navy-light) remain unchanged and theme-invariant
html, body, #root use var(--color-page) for background and var(--color-text-primary) for color
No Tailwind build errors or CSS variable resolution failures

tasks.title: Implement useTheme hook (system preference + localStorage + toggle)
tasks.description: Create src/hooks/useTheme.ts. The hook reads localStorage key 'optcg-theme', resolves system preference via matchMedia, applies html.light or html.dark class to document.documentElement, listens for matchMedia changes when in 'system' mode, and exposes { theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme }. See blueprint section 2.3 for full algorithm.
acceptance_criteria: On first load with no localStorage entry, theme resolves from system preference (prefers-color-scheme)
setTheme('light') writes 'light' to localStorage and applies html.light class
setTheme('dark') writes 'dark' to localStorage and applies html.dark class
setTheme('system') writes 'system' to localStorage and resolves from matchMedia
When in 'system' mode, changing OS preference updates resolvedTheme reactively
document.documentElement always has exactly one of 'light' or 'dark' classes, never both, never neither
Hook can be called from multiple components without conflicting state

tasks.title: Build ThemeToggle component
tasks.description: Create src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx. Renders a button with Material Symbol icons (light_mode / dark_mode) that cycles through system -> light -> dark -> system. Uses useTheme hook. Tooltip shows current mode. See blueprint section 2.2.
acceptance_criteria: Renders a button with 'light_mode' icon when resolvedTheme is light, 'dark_mode' when dark
Clicking cycles: system -> light -> dark -> system
Has aria-label indicating current theme mode
Has title/tooltip showing 'System', 'Light', or 'Dark'
Uses semantic token classes (not hardcoded dark/light colors)
Focus-visible ring uses optcg-gold

tasks.title: Build LandingPage component
tasks.description: Create src/components/LandingPage.tsx. Full-viewport centered layout with app logo ('ONE PIECE TCG Card Search' in Roboto Slab), a search form (text input + submit button), and a subtitle. On form submit, navigate to /search?q=... using react-router-dom useNavigate. Include ThemeToggle in top-right. See blueprint section 2.1.
acceptance_criteria: Renders at / route path
Displays logo text in Roboto Slab (font-slab class)
Has a search input and submit button centered vertically and horizontally
On Enter or button click, navigates to /search?q=<encoded-value>
Does NOT call loadCards() or interact with useCardStore
Does NOT mount useCardUrlSync
ThemeToggle is positioned in the top-right corner
Uses semantic token classes (bg-page, text-primary, etc.)
Looks correct in both light and dark modes
Empty input submission navigates to /search (no q param) — this shows all cards

tasks.title: Update router — / -> LandingPage, /search -> SearchPage
tasks.description: Modify src/App.tsx to import LandingPage and SearchPage (renamed). Route / to LandingPage, /search to SearchPage, /cards redirects to /search, * redirects to /. Remove the old CardSearchPage import. See blueprint section 1.
acceptance_criteria: / renders LandingPage
/search renders SearchPage
/cards redirects to /search
/* redirects to /
No references to CardSearchPage remain in App.tsx
Direct URL /search?q=Luffy loads SearchPage with correct query

tasks.title: Update SearchBar — on submit navigates to /search?q=...
tasks.description: Modify src/components/SearchBar.tsx to replace hardcoded dark classes with semantic token classes per the blueprint section 3.2 mapping. No behavioral changes needed — SearchBar already updates the store via debounce, and useCardUrlSync handles URL sync. The landing page has its own separate search input.
acceptance_criteria: All hardcoded dark classes replaced per blueprint section 3.2
SearchBar renders correctly in both light and dark mode
Debounced search still works (type -> results update after 200ms)
Clear button still works
/ keyboard shortcut still works
Escape key still clears and blurs
No visual regression in dark mode compared to current design

tasks.title: Rename CardSearchPage -> SearchPage, adapt for URL-driven initial query
tasks.description: Rename src/components/CardSearchPage.tsx to src/components/SearchPage.tsx. Update the export name to SearchPage. Replace all hardcoded dark classes with semantic token classes per blueprint section 3.1. Add ThemeToggle to the sticky header bar (right-aligned). No changes to useCardUrlSync or loadCards — they already handle URL-driven queries.
acceptance_criteria: File renamed from CardSearchPage.tsx to SearchPage.tsx
Export renamed from CardSearchPage to SearchPage
All hardcoded dark classes replaced with semantic tokens
ThemeToggle appears in the sticky header bar, right-aligned
useCardUrlSync still mounted and functional
loadCards() still called on mount
CardGridSkeleton uses bg-input instead of bg-optcg-navy-light
Renders correctly in both light and dark mode
All internal imports updated (no broken references)

tasks.title: Audit + update all components for theme-aware classes
tasks.description: Systematically update every remaining component file to replace hardcoded dark classes with semantic token classes. Components: CardThumbnail, CardDetailModal, CardRow, CardStats, CardEffectText, ResultsCount, ViewToggle, CardImage, FilterPanel, and all 6 filter sub-components (ColorFilter, TypeFilter, RangeFilter, CounterFilter, SetFilter, AttributeFilter). Also update shared/Modal.tsx to ensure its existing token references (--color-surface, --color-border) flip correctly. Use the mapping table in blueprint sections 3.3 through 3.13.
acceptance_criteria: Zero remaining references to text-white (except inside theme-invariant contexts like cost pips)
Zero remaining references to border-white/ (replaced with border-border or border-card-border)
Zero remaining references to bg-optcg-navy as a page/section background (replaced with bg-page)
Zero remaining references to bg-optcg-navy-light as an input/card background (replaced with bg-input or bg-card)
CardThumbnail cost pip and life pip retain gold/navy colors (theme-invariant)
CardDetailModal art panel keeps bg-black/20 regardless of theme
OPTCG color indicators (red/blue/green/yellow/purple/black dots) unchanged in both themes
Type badges (Leader=gold, Character=blue, Event=purple, Stage=green) maintain correct contrast in both themes
Modal backdrop and glass effects work in both themes
Every component visually inspected in both light and dark mode

tasks.title: Update App.tsx — apply theme class to <html> on mount + inline script
tasks.description: Add the inline <script> to index.html <head> that synchronously reads localStorage and applies html.light or html.dark class before React hydrates (prevents flash). Mount useTheme() at the App level (or inside LandingPage and SearchPage individually — since both already include ThemeToggle which calls useTheme). Verify no flash of wrong theme on hard reload.
acceptance_criteria: index.html contains inline <script> in <head> that sets html.light or html.dark before body renders
Hard refresh with localStorage set to 'dark' shows dark theme immediately (no flash of light)
Hard refresh with localStorage set to 'light' shows light theme immediately (no flash of dark)
Hard refresh with no localStorage entry resolves from system preference with no flash
The inline script is fewer than 10 lines and has no external dependencies

tasks.title: Verify all tests pass, update snapshots if needed
tasks.description: Run the full test suite (vitest run). Fix any broken imports (CardSearchPage -> SearchPage rename). Update snapshot tests if class names changed. Ensure coverage remains above existing baseline. Test both theme modes manually in the browser.
acceptance_criteria: vitest run passes with zero failures
No broken import references to CardSearchPage
Snapshot files updated if class names changed
Manual browser test: landing page renders at /
Manual browser test: search from landing page navigates to /search?q=...
Manual browser test: /search?q=Luffy loads correct results
Manual browser test: theme toggle cycles correctly and persists across reload
Manual browser test: system preference detection works (toggle OS dark mode)
Manual browser test: all components look correct in light mode
Manual browser test: all components look correct in dark mode (no visual regression from current design)
